briefing.opening: Hello everyone. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 11, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis: A Threat Becoming Reality
sections.content: Globally, the direct impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly evident. Glaciers in the Everest region are melting, increasing climbing risks [2], while wild blueberry farms in Maine, USA, and fruit farms in Gyeongsangbuk-do, South Korea, are suffering due to changes in cultivation periods and pest issues caused by climate change [3, 30]. Furthermore, the Phoenix area in the United States is experiencing unusual heat in May [40], and an overwhelming majority of Colorado residents state that climate change harms human health [33]. France's tourism sector is exacerbating the climate crisis by emitting 75 million tons of CO₂ [59], and agriculture faces a triple crisis of war, climate change, and inflation [55]. These phenomena clearly demonstrate that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern but a reality directly impacting various aspects of our lives.

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy: Transition Through Innovation
sections.content: Efforts towards carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition to address the climate crisis have become a global mega-trend, with a former UN Under-Secretary-General stating that renewable energy is no longer an option but a necessity [7, 18]. Germany is accelerating its industrial transition to carbon neutrality by approving industrial decarbonization contracts worth $5.9 billion [11], and domestically, Chungcheongnam-do is encouraging community participation by expanding eligibility for the Carbon Neutrality Point System and operating 'Carbon Up-Shoe' [8, 9]. Particularly noteworthy is the change in the industrial sector, where KT signed a PPA agreement to directly supply 50MW of renewable energy to Tokyo Electron Korea, creating the largest renewable energy supply case among global semiconductor equipment companies [14, 21, 23, 25]. Furthermore, as of May 2026, with the advent of the 'Carbon-Neutral AI Era,' AI-RE100 is being recognized as a new competitive factor [15], and the role of the financial sector is also growing, with Shinhan Bank, Shinhan Asset Management, and Hyosung Heavy Industries expanding their global renewable energy business collaborations [20, 24].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Climate Action by Future Generations and Local Communities
sections.content: Responding to the climate crisis is becoming stronger through the active participation of not only governments and corporations but also civil society, especially future generations. Domestically, elementary school students are practicing climate change response activities through 'Leftover ZERO' campaigns and using personal water bottles [1], and the Daejeon Regional Meteorological Administration and Suncheon Bay Eco-Cultural Education Center are also raising awareness among future generations by operating climate crisis response contests and education for elementary school students [5, 13]. Local governments are also collaborating with the Korea Forest Service to achieve carbon neutrality [10], and Wonju City is exploring region-specific policies, such as conducting a demand survey for its renewable energy convergence support project [26]. Concurrently, a 79.5% increase in umbrella sales at GS25 convenience stores is an example demonstrating that public awareness of climate change is leading to changes in consumption patterns [4].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Policies and Perceptions
sections.content: The international community continues multifaceted discussions and responses to climate change. The IPCC has begun reviewing the draft of its 'Climate Change and Cities' report, highlighting challenges for South Korean urban policy [6], and according to a Brookings study, care services are analyzed to support effective climate adaptation within national adaptation plans and greenhouse gas reduction targets [41]. Meanwhile, many African nations have expressed the stance that they should not be forced to choose between development financing and climate change response, emphasizing the importance of a just transition [50]. A survey of global civil servants indicated that interest in AI and climate change are expected to emerge as key issues in 2026 [51], however, differences in perception exist, with Canadians expressing greater concern for economic growth than climate change [56]. These international discussions suggest that responding to the climate crisis is a complex challenge spanning social, economic, and political spheres, beyond merely an environmental issue.

briefing.closing: Today's climate briefing has shown that while the global climate crisis is deepening, innovative efforts and community participation to overcome it are also actively underway. This is a time when sustained attention and multifaceted responses to climate change are becoming even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: LS Cable & System and KEPCO E&C are embarking on a strategic collaboration to secure a leading position in the rapidly growing floating offshore wind market. The plan is to maximize global competitiveness by combining the capabilities of both companies from the design stage. LS Cable & System announced on the 11th that it signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with KEPCO E&C for floating offshore wind cooperation. The core of this agreement is to integrate from the initial stages of power system design...

summary.phenomenon: LS Cable & System and KEPCO E&C signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for floating offshore wind cooperation on the 11th. Both companies will engage in strategic collaboration on core design and procurement technologies to secure a leading position in the floating offshore wind market.
summary.outlook: Both companies plan to maximize global competitiveness by combining their capabilities from the design stage.

articles.title: Lasers in the sky: hi-tech missions track record snowpack loss in US west
articles.description: Data from missions showing critically low snowpack on mountains across the west raises alarm among expertsHigh above the jagged peaks of California’s Sierra Nevada, the view from the cockpit is breathtaking. At first glance, the mountains appear draped in a pristine white blanket. But as the flight crew gears up for a high-stakes mission, the sensors onboard this specialized aircraft prove that looks can be deceiving.“This is a distinct dry year,” says Tom Painter, CEO of Airborne Snow Observato

summary.phenomenon: Record-low snowpack has been observed in the mountainous regions of the Western United States, particularly California's Sierra Nevada range, through aerial sensor data.
summary.outlook: Experts are expressing concern over the critically low snowpack in the Western U.S. mountainous regions, which could have future implications.
